things.name: McLean Museum & Art Gallery
things.description: Greenock's free museum with collections covering James Watt's inventions, local maritime history and an impressive art collection

things.name: Greenock Cut Reservoir Walk
things.description: Spectacular 10-mile Victorian aqueduct walk high above the Clyde — built in 1827 to supply drinking water, with stunning estuary panoramas

things.name: Battery Park
things.description: Greenock's main park on the Clyde waterfront with an outdoor pool, children's rides and sweeping views across to the Argyll hills

things.name: Custom House Museum
things.description: Elegant neoclassical Custom House on the waterfront housing a maritime heritage museum about Greenock's ship-building past

things.name: Lunderston Bay Beach
things.description: Popular shingle beach at the head of the Firth of Clyde — a beautiful spot for rock-pooling and watching yachts sail past the Cloch Lighthouse

things.name: James Watt Birthplace & Memorial
things.description: Commemorating Greenock's most famous son, inventor of the modern steam engine — a statue and heritage trail around his birthplace

things.name: Inverkip Marina
things.description: One of Scotland's largest marinas in a sheltered bay — beautiful sailing waters with boat hire, leisure facilities and sea views

things.name: Gourock Outdoor Swimming Pool
things.description: Scotland's only heated outdoor seawater lido — an iconic Art Deco pool on the Clyde waterfront open May to September

things.name: Clyde Muirshiel Regional Park
things.description: Scotland's largest regional park stretching from Greenock to Irvine — moorland, reservoirs and wildlife including red deer

things.name: Glasgow (Day Trip via train)
things.description: Scotland's largest city just 45 minutes by train — world-class museums, Burrell Collection, Mackintosh buildings and a thriving food scene
